scheme_name: Promoting to Farmer for Post Harvesting & Management (Value Addition)
page_title: Promoting to Farmer for Post Harvesting & Management (Value Addition)

details: The scheme "Promoting to Farmer for Post Harvesting & Management (Value Addition)" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to encourage all categories of farmers to establish crop value addition units that utilize post-harvesting technology and management strategies.
benefits: Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits
50% of the cost or ₹10,00,000/- whichever is less
eligibility: Self-Help Groups (SHGs) and Sakhi Mandals of Women farmers
Eligible Individuals with relevant agricultural education:

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at I-Khedut Portal:
On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button and an application page will open.
Click on "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a print out of the confirmed application.
Applicant can also check their Application Status/View/Print/Upload Application details from the below link:
Application will be accepted on the principle of first come, first serve bases within the target limits.

faqs.question: What is the objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to support farmers in setting up crop value addition units using post-harvesting technology and management concepts. It encourages farmers to enhance agricultural productivity and profitability by adopting value addition techniques.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Every category of farmers, including individual farmers, farmer groups, women farmers, farmer producer groups, cooperative societies, and farmers' interest groups, is eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides financial assistance of 50% of the project cost or up to ₹10,00,000/-, whichever is lower.

faqs.question: What is the minimum duration for maintaining the project to retain benefits?
faqs.answer: The beneficiary must maintain the project for at least 10 years to continue receiving benefits.

faqs.question: Can a woman farmer apply for the scheme individually?
faqs.answer: Yes, a woman farmer can apply individually, and women-led self-help groups (Sakhi Mandal) are also eligible.

faqs.question: Where can an applicant apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: An applicant can apply online through the iKhedut portal at https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in.

faqs.question: How are applications processed under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applications are processed on a first-come, first-serve basis within the target limit.

faqs.question: Who should an applicant contact for assistance with the application process?
faqs.answer: An applicant can contact the nearest agriculture department office or visit the iKhedut portal for support.
